info.name: COA RemoteRest - Weighted Supplier Validation v2
info.description:
USPS IVS RemoteRest - COA Weighted Supplier Selection Validation

PURPOSE
Validates that the WeightedSupplierSelector refactor routes traffic correctly
across LexisNexis, Equifax IDFS, and Equifax SMFA without updating
RP_KBA_ATTEMPTS.ATTEMPTS in the database.

FOLDER STRUCTURE
  01 - Runner Safe: CheckDevice + VerifyPhone only.
       Use Collection Runner for 20/50/100 iterations.
       Safe because it never calls ConfirmPasscode or ValidateLink.
  02 - Manual Full Flow: CheckDevice, VerifyPhone, then ValidateLink OR
       ConfirmPasscode depending on the detected supplier path.
       Run requests one at a time and read the console before proceeding.

HOW TO VALIDATE SUPPLIER DISTRIBUTION
  1. Run Folder 01 with 50-100 iterations in Collection Runner.
  2. Watch the WebSphere application logs for lines matching:
       'RefOtpSupplier determineVerificationMethod selectedSupplier:'
     Count how many times each supplier appears.
  3. After all iterations, run this SQL against Oracle:
       SELECT KBA_SUPPLIER_ID, ATTEMPTS, TOTAL_ATTEMPTS
       FROM RP_KBA_ATTEMPTS
       WHERE SPONSOR_ID = (SELECT SPONSOR_ID FROM REF_SPONSOR WHERE SPONSOR_NAME = 'Change of Address')
       ORDER BY KBA_SUPPLIER_ID;
     EXPECTED: ATTEMPTS column must NOT have changed from its value before the test.
     If ATTEMPTS did NOT change, the WeightedSupplierSelector fix is working.
     If ATTEMPTS DID change, callingOTP() is still being invoked — investigate.

CUSTOMER UNIQUE ID RULES
  - Numeric-only (no underscores, dashes, or special characters).
  - Generated ONCE per flow in the CheckDevice pre-request script.
  - Reused unchanged in VerifyPhone, ValidateLink, and ConfirmPasscode.
  - A new ID is generated for each new iteration (CheckDevice pre-request runs every time).

SUPPLIER PATH DETECTION
  Because RemoteResponse does not include a supplierId field directly,
  path detection reads the 'reasonCode' field in the JSON response:
    SuccessfulSMFADelivery   -> EQUIFAX_SMFA  -> run ValidateLink next
    SuccessfulOTPDelivery    -> OTP_LEXIS_OR_EQUIFAX_IDFS -> run ConfirmPasscode next
    ApprovedPhoneVerification-> APPROVED_NO_OTP -> flow complete, no next step
    FailedPhoneVerification  -> FAILED -> do not run ConfirmPasscode or ValidateLink
  If reasonCode is empty or unrecognised, read application logs before proceeding.

PREREQUISITES
  - Set 'jagan-cell' collection variable to a real 10-digit mobile number (no dashes).
  - Set 'local-ips' to your local WebSphere host:port (default http://localhost:9080).
  - WebSphere and Oracle must be running locally.
  - The COA sponsor must be configured in REF_SPONSOR.
  - RP_KBA_ATTEMPTS rows for the COA sponsor must exist with non-zero TOTAL_ATTEMPTS.

variable.description: WebSphere host and port. Change to match your local environment.
REQUIRED. Your 10-digit mobile number (numeric, no dashes). Receives OTP or SMFA link.
Auto-generated per flow in CheckDevice pre-request. Do NOT set manually.
Enter the OTP received on jagan-cell before running ConfirmPasscode.
Captured from VerifyPhone response. Used for debugging only.
Derived from reasonCode in VerifyPhone response. Drives next-step guidance.
Raw reasonCode from VerifyPhone response. Captured for log correlation.

item.name: 01 - Runner Safe - Supplier Selection Validation
item.description:
USE THIS FOLDER WITH COLLECTION RUNNER.

Run 20, 50, or 100 iterations. Each iteration:
  1. Generates a fresh numeric customerUniqueID in CheckDevice pre-request.
  2. Calls CheckDevice (device reputation).
  3. Calls VerifyPhone (triggers WeightedSupplierSelector).
  4. Logs customerUniqueID, reasonCode, and detected supplier path to the Postman console.

This folder does NOT call ConfirmPasscode or ValidateLink, so it is safe to run
repeatedly without hitting OTP send limits.

AFTER RUNNING: validate supplier distribution via application logs and SQL.
See collection description for the SQL query and expected results.

item.name: 01.1 - CheckDevice - Generate Customer ID
item.description:
Generates a unique numeric customerUniqueID for this iteration.
Calls CheckDevice to establish a device reputation record for the user.
customerUniqueID is set here and must NOT be regenerated in subsequent requests.

exec: // Generate customerUniqueID ONCE per COA flow.
// Rules: numeric-only, no underscores or special characters.
// Format: 7 trailing digits of epoch ms + 3 random digits = 10 digits.
// This runs at the START of every iteration in Collection Runner.
pm.expect(id).to.match(/^\d+$/, 'customerUniqueID must be digits only, got: ' + id);

item.name: 01.2 - VerifyPhone - Trigger Supplier Selection
item.description:
Calls VerifyPhone using the customerUniqueID generated in 01.1.
Do NOT modify this request body to change customerUniqueID.

This request triggers WeightedSupplierSelector.select() in the application.
The supplier chosen is visible in the WebSphere application log:
  'RefOtpSupplier determineVerificationMethod selectedSupplier: <name>'

The post-test script reads reasonCode from the response to detect the supplier path.
Since RemoteResponse does not return a supplierId field, reasonCode is the
most reliable signal available without reading application logs.

DB VALIDATION after running 50+ iterations:
  SELECT KBA_SUPPLIER_ID, ATTEMPTS, TOTAL_ATTEMPTS
  FROM RP_KBA_ATTEMPTS
  WHERE SPONSOR_ID = (SELECT SPONSOR_ID FROM REF_SPONSOR
                      WHERE SPONSOR_NAME = 'Change of Address')
  ORDER BY KBA_SUPPLIER_ID;
  EXPECTED: ATTEMPTS must NOT change. If it does, callingOTP() is still being invoked.

exec: // GUARD: customerUniqueID must have been set by CheckDevice.
// Do NOT regenerate it here. If it is empty, the flow was
// started incorrectly — abort and run CheckDevice first.
throw new Error('[VerifyPhone] customerUniqueID is empty. Run CheckDevice first to generate it.');
throw new Error('[VerifyPhone] customerUniqueID is not numeric-only: ' + id);
pm.test('VerifyPhone: status is not a server error (5xx)', function () {
console.warn('[VerifyPhone] Response body is not JSON: ' + responseText);
// RemoteResponse does NOT include a supplierId field in its JSON.
// The reasonCode is the most reliable programmatic signal available:
//   'SuccessfulOTPDelivery'     -> LexisNexis OTP or Equifax IDFS OTP selected.
//                                  Next step: flow is complete, no further calls needed.
//                                  Next step: do not run ConfirmPasscode or ValidateLink.
//   Any other value             -> Read application logs to determine path.
// To determine the EXACT supplier (LexisNexis vs Equifax IDFS on the OTP path),
console.log('[VerifyPhone] --> Equifax SMFA path. Run ValidateLink next. Check jagan-cell for the secure link.');
console.log('[VerifyPhone] --> OTP path. Run ConfirmPasscode next. Check jagan-cell for the OTP code.');
console.log('[VerifyPhone] --> To confirm whether LexisNexis or Equifax IDFS was used, search app log for: selectedSupplier:');
console.log('[VerifyPhone] --> Phone approved without OTP. Flow is complete.');
console.log('[VerifyPhone] --> Verification failed. Do NOT run ConfirmPasscode or ValidateLink.');
console.warn('[VerifyPhone] --> Path UNKNOWN. Read application logs before next step.');
pm.test('customerUniqueID was NOT regenerated by VerifyPhone', function () {
pm.expect(id).to.match(/^\d+$/, 'customerUniqueID must remain numeric and unchanged');

item.name: 02 - Manual Full Flow - One Request at a Time
item.description:
USE THIS FOLDER FOR MANUAL, STEP-BY-STEP TESTING.

Do NOT use Collection Runner on this folder.
Run each request one at a time and read the Postman console before continuing.

FLOW:
  Step 1: Run 02.1 CheckDevice   -> generates customerUniqueID
  Step 2: Run 02.2 VerifyPhone   -> read console to determine path
  Step 3a: If EQUIFAX_SMFA:      -> tap the link on jagan-cell, then run 02.3 ValidateLink
  Step 3b: If OTP path:          -> enter OTP from jagan-cell into 'passcode' variable, then run 02.4 ConfirmPasscode
  Step 3c: If APPROVED_NO_OTP:   -> flow is complete, do nothing
  Step 3d: If FAILED:            -> do not run 02.3 or 02.4

DO NOT run both ValidateLink AND ConfirmPasscode for the same flow.
DO NOT modify customerUniqueID after it is generated in 02.1.

DB VALIDATION:
  After any full flow, check:
    SELECT KBA_SUPPLIER_ID, ATTEMPTS, TOTAL_ATTEMPTS
    FROM RP_KBA_ATTEMPTS
    WHERE SPONSOR_ID = (SELECT SPONSOR_ID FROM REF_SPONSOR
                        WHERE SPONSOR_NAME = 'Change of Address')
    ORDER BY KBA_SUPPLIER_ID;
  EXPECTED: ATTEMPTS must NOT have incremented. If it did, the fix is not active.

item.name: 02.1 - CheckDevice - Start Flow
item.description:
Generates customerUniqueID for this manual flow.
Resets all flow-tracking variables.

exec: // Generate a fresh customerUniqueID for this manual flow.
console.log('[02.1 CheckDevice] --> Next step: run 02.2 VerifyPhone.');

item.name: 02.2 - VerifyPhone - Triggers Supplier Selection
item.description:
Uses the customerUniqueID from 02.1. Do NOT regenerate it.
Read the console output before deciding whether to run ValidateLink or ConfirmPasscode.
birtDate is required by RemoteProofingValidation (format MMddyyyy).

exec: // GUARD: customerUniqueID must already exist from 02.1 CheckDevice.
throw new Error('[02.2 VerifyPhone] customerUniqueID is empty. Run 02.1 CheckDevice first.');
throw new Error('[02.2 VerifyPhone] customerUniqueID contains non-numeric characters: ' + id);
pm.test('VerifyPhone: status is not a server error', function () {
console.warn('[02.2 VerifyPhone] Response is not JSON: ' + responseText);
// Path detection via reasonCode (see collection description for full mapping).
console.log('[02.2 VerifyPhone] verified=' + verified + ', passcodeSent=' + passcodeSent);
console.log('NEXT STEP: Check jagan-cell for the Equifax secure link. Tap the link on your phone.');
console.log('NEXT STEP: Check jagan-cell for the OTP SMS code.');
console.log('           Set the passcode collection variable to that code.');
console.log('           To confirm which supplier (LexisNexis vs Equifax IDFS), check app log for:');
console.log('NEXT STEP: Phone approved without OTP. Flow is complete. No further requests needed.');
console.log('NEXT STEP: Verification failed. Do NOT run ValidateLink or ConfirmPasscode.');
console.log('           Check application logs for the failure reason.');
console.warn('NEXT STEP: Path is UNKNOWN. Read application logs before proceeding.');

item.name: 02.3 - ValidateLink - Equifax SMFA Path Only
item.description:
Run ONLY if 02.2 VerifyPhone detected EQUIFAX_SMFA path (reasonCode=SuccessfulSMFADelivery).
Do NOT run for OTP/LexisNexis path.
The pre-request script aborts the request if selectedSupplierPath is not EQUIFAX_SMFA.

exec: // GUARD: ValidateLink is ONLY for Equifax SMFA path.
// If the detected path is anything else, stop the request.
throw new Error('[02.3 ValidateLink] customerUniqueID is empty. Run 02.1 CheckDevice first.');
const msg = '[02.3 ValidateLink] BLOCKED. selectedSupplierPath="' + path + '".' +
' ValidateLink is only valid for path=EQUIFAX_SMFA.' +
' If you intended to run this, check VerifyPhone response first.';
console.log('[02.3 ValidateLink] Path confirmed as EQUIFAX_SMFA. Proceeding.');
console.log('[02.3 ValidateLink] Make sure you have already tapped the secure link on jagan-cell.');
pm.test('ValidateLink: status is not a server error', function () {
console.log('[02.3 ValidateLink] reasonCode=' + reasonCode + ', verified=' + verified);
console.log('[02.3 ValidateLink] SMFA validated successfully. Flow complete.');
console.warn('[02.3 ValidateLink] SMFA not yet validated. Link may still be pending. Check jagan-cell and retry if needed.');

item.name: 02.4 - ConfirmPasscode - OTP Path Only
item.description:
Run ONLY if 02.2 VerifyPhone detected OTP_LEXIS_OR_EQUIFAX_IDFS path (reasonCode=SuccessfulOTPDelivery).
Do NOT run for Equifax SMFA path.
Set the 'passcode' collection variable to the OTP you received on jagan-cell before running this.
The pre-request script aborts if selectedSupplierPath is EQUIFAX_SMFA.

exec: // GUARD: ConfirmPasscode is NOT for Equifax SMFA path.
throw new Error('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] customerUniqueID is empty. Run 02.1 CheckDevice first.');
' ConfirmPasscode is not valid for the SMFA path. Run 02.3 ValidateLink instead.';
console.warn('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] passcode collection variable is empty.');
console.warn('Set it to the OTP from jagan-cell before running this request.');
console.warn('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] WARNING: path=APPROVED_NO_OTP. Phone was already approved. ConfirmPasscode is likely unnecessary.');
console.warn('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] WARNING: path=' + path + '. VerifyPhone failed. ConfirmPasscode will likely also fail.');
pm.test('ConfirmPasscode: status is not a server error', function () {
console.log('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] confirmationAttempts=' + attempts + ', limitReached=' + limitReached);
console.log('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] OTP confirmed successfully. LOA achieved. Flow complete.');
console.warn('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] Wrong passcode. Attempts=' + attempts + '. Retry with correct OTP if limit not reached.');
console.error('[02.4 ConfirmPasscode] OTP attempt limit reached. Flow is locked.');
